plan.failureSummary: The test failed during a cached 'click' action on a '×' (close) button that no longer exists or has changed on the homepage.
observedIndicators: Failure on '×' button click which is not part of the explicit test instructions but likely a cached interstitial action
History shows alternating SUCCESS/FAILED pattern, suggesting a recurring cache staleness or UI flakiness issue
Failure screenshot shows the homepage where the action was attempted, but the target element is missing or changed compared to what the deterministic cache expects

remediationSteps.details: Execute the test again with the 'clearPageAiCache' directive. This will force the autonomous agent to re-scan the page and ignore the stale cached selector for the '×' button.

remediationSteps.summary: Allow AI to self-heal the selector during retry.
remediationSteps.details: If the '×' button is a persistent banner that changed its DOM structure, the AI will naturally pick up the new selector during the retry. No manual code change is expected to be necessary.

plan.notes: The failure occurred in DETERMINISTIC mode. The recorded flow attempted to click a 'close' button (selector: //button[normalize-space(.)='×']), likely associated with a promotional banner or popup present in previous runs. The failure screenshot shows the Checkly homepage with a 'Rocky AI' banner but no obvious '×' button matching the cached selector. Because this is the first attempt (not a retry), clearing the cache to allow the AI to re-evaluate the page structure is the most effective fix.
